sg_weights._note: Shinnecock Hills: Links-style major with firm, fast greens and thick native rough. APP heavily elevated (small greens, demanding angles). ARG elevated (thick rough around greens demands recovery). PUTT moderate (fast poa/bentgrass but not primary differentiator). OTT standard (wind matters but accuracy over distance).

course_weight_override._note: Major championship at historic links-style venue. Course fit layer has HIGH confidence — Shinnecock rewards a specific player archetype (precision irons, scrambling, wind play).

course_history_note: U.S. Open rotates venues annually. Historical rounds indexed under event_id=26 across all host courses. Shinnecock Hills hosted in 2018 and 2004. Limited direct course history — model relies on general US Open form and player major championship performance.
winner_archetype: Complete ball-striker with elite scrambling. Shinnecock Hills demands precision iron play into small, firm greens and the ability to recover from thick native rough. Historical winners and contenders at Shinnecock include Koepka, Johnson, Rose — players with strong all-around games and major championship experience. Wind tolerance and creative shotmaking are critical differentiators.
bbi_pipeline_action: FULL — Major championship. Shinnecock Hills is a links-style test with firm conditions and thick rough. Elevated courseFit weights. Phase 1 should return high field coverage for the US Open field.
course_notes: Shinnecock Hills Golf Club, Southampton NY. Par-70, 7445y. One of the most historic and demanding venues in American golf. Hosted US Opens in 2004 and 2018. Firm, fast poa/bentgrass greens at 13.0+ stimp. Thick native rough (1.5in+) penalizes errant shots. Exposed to Atlantic winds. Small, contoured green complexes demand precise approach play. Creative shotmaking and scrambling are essential.
